title: JIT performance benchmarks and adaptive compilation tuning
purpose: Validate that JIT compilation provides measurable speedup over interpretation and tune the compilation threshold. Criterion benchmarks compare JIT-compiled vs interpreted execution for representative Gleam workload patterns: recursive compute (fibonacci), collection processing (map/filter/fold), and pattern matching (multi-clause case expressions). The results inform the default compilation threshold and provide a regression tracking baseline. Per the design goal: hot BEAM functions compile to native code with no observable behaviour change versus interpretation.
task: Create Criterion benchmarks in crates/beamr/benches/jit_comparison.rs that measure interpreted vs compiled execution for three representative workloads. Implement adaptive threshold tuning in the JitProfiler that adjusts the compilation threshold based on observed compilation latency and speedup. The default threshold (1000 calls per B-130 R5) may be adjusted based on benchmark data.

requirements.title: Criterion benchmark — fibonacci recursive compute
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a Criterion benchmark that computes fibonacci(30) in both interpreted and JIT-compiled mode and measures the execution time. THE SYSTEM SHALL run the JIT-compiled version by pre-compiling the fibonacci function (bypassing the profiler threshold) before benchmarking. THE SYSTEM SHALL report the speedup factor (interpreted_time / compiled_time) in the console output via a Criterion custom measurement or post-benchmark summary.
acceptance: bench_fibonacci_interpreted: runs fibonacci(30) via the interpreter
bench_fibonacci_compiled: pre-compiles fibonacci, then runs via native code
Both benchmarks produce the same result (correctness check before timing)
Criterion statistical output shows mean, median, and standard deviation
Test: both benchmarks produce fibonacci(30) = 832040

requirements.title: Criterion benchmark — list processing (map/filter/fold)
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a Criterion benchmark that applies map, filter, and fold over a 10,000-element list in both interpreted and JIT-compiled mode. THE SYSTEM SHALL construct the BEAM bytecode for a function that maps (x -> x * 2), filters (x > 5000), and folds (sum) the list.
acceptance: bench_list_processing_interpreted: map/filter/fold over 10K list via interpreter
bench_list_processing_compiled: same operation via pre-compiled native code
Both benchmarks produce the same result
Benchmark constructs the test list and BEAM bytecode in the setup phase (not timed)
Test: both benchmarks produce the same final sum

requirements.title: Criterion benchmark — pattern matching intensive
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a Criterion benchmark that executes a function with a 20-clause case expression, dispatching to each clause in round-robin across 10,000 iterations. This benchmarks select_val jump tables vs interpreter switch dispatch.
acceptance: bench_pattern_match_interpreted: 20-clause case * 10K iterations via interpreter
bench_pattern_match_compiled: same via pre-compiled native code (select_val becomes jump table)
Both benchmarks produce the same result
The 20 clauses cover: 10 integer matches, 5 atom matches, 5 tuple tag matches
Test: both benchmarks produce the same dispatch count per clause

requirements.title: Compilation latency benchmark
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a Criterion benchmark that measures the time to JIT-compile each of the three workload functions (fibonacci, list processing, pattern match). This establishes the compilation cost that the threshold amortises. THE SYSTEM SHALL report the compilation time as a standalone metric, not included in the execution benchmarks.
acceptance: bench_compile_fibonacci: measures JitCompiler::compile time for the fibonacci function
bench_compile_list_processing: measures compile time for the list processing function
bench_compile_pattern_match: measures compile time for the 20-clause function
Compilation benchmarks are in a separate Criterion group from execution benchmarks
Test: each compilation benchmark completes without error

requirements.title: Adaptive threshold tuning
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide JitProfiler::tune_threshold(compilation_time_us: u64, speedup_factor: f64) that adjusts the compilation threshold based on observed compilation cost and speedup. WHEN compilation is fast and speedup is high, THE SYSTEM SHALL lower the threshold (compile sooner). WHEN compilation is slow and speedup is low, THE SYSTEM SHALL raise the threshold (compile less eagerly). THE SYSTEM SHALL clamp the threshold between a minimum of 100 and a maximum of 10,000 calls.
acceptance: JitProfiler::tune_threshold(compilation_time_us, speedup_factor) adjusts the internal threshold
Threshold decreases when speedup_factor > 2.0 and compilation_time_us < 10_000
Threshold increases when speedup_factor < 1.5 or compilation_time_us > 100_000
Threshold is clamped to [100, 10_000]
JitProfiler::current_threshold() returns the current threshold value
Test: tune_threshold with fast compile + high speedup -> threshold decreases
Test: tune_threshold with slow compile + low speedup -> threshold increases
Test: threshold never goes below 100 or above 10_000

requirements.title: Default threshold validation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL set the default compilation threshold to 1000 (per B-130 R5). THE SYSTEM SHALL document in a comment on the default constant that the value was chosen to amortise Cranelift compilation cost for functions called in tight loops, and that tune_threshold may adjust it at runtime. IF benchmark results show that a different default is more appropriate, THE SYSTEM SHALL update the default and note the rationale.
acceptance: DEFAULT_JIT_THRESHOLD constant is 1000
SchedulerConfig::default() uses DEFAULT_JIT_THRESHOLD when jit_threshold is None
The constant is referenced by both SchedulerConfig and JitProfiler
Test: SchedulerConfig::default() creates a profiler with threshold 1000

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement profile-guided optimisation (PGO) — simple call counting with adaptive threshold only
SHALL NOT implement tiered compilation (multiple optimisation levels) — single Cranelift tier
SHALL NOT implement continuous re-tuning in production — tune_threshold is called after benchmark analysis, not on every compilation
SHALL NOT include compilation time in execution benchmarks — compilation is pre-warmed
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
cargo bench -p beamr runs all JIT comparison benchmarks to completion
Each benchmark pair (interpreted vs compiled) produces identical results
